plans.title: F&O Multi-Signal Expansion — 3 Walk-Forward-Validated LONG Systems
plans.subtitle: Walk-forward PASSED on Donchian 55, ADX>30, RSI<40-pullback (LONG only). All 24 SHORT variants DOA. Spread-structure layer already deployed — winners auto-inherit bull-call generation. Wiring deferred — start observing paper signals first.

sections.title: Why this is parked, not deployed
sections.body: All three winners are LONG trend-continuation breakouts on the same 76-stock F&O universe that research/21 (252-day Donchian, already live as system_id='fno') already trades. Before adding 3 more system_ids and getting up to ~30 paper signals/day, we want a few weeks of data to measure per-day signal overlap with the live FNO system — if Donchian-55 fires on the same day as research/21 on 80% of trades, it's not a separate system, it's just an earlier trigger.

sections.title: What was validated (OOS = 2023-01-01 to 2025-12-31)
sections.body: Pass criteria: PF >= 1.20 AND Sharpe >= 0.8 AND MaxDD <= 30%.

items.label: Donchian 55 — 55_vol_3x_atr_floor (top pick)
items.body: PF 1.97, Sharpe 1.25, MaxDD 7.74%, CAGR +13.47%, n=175 OOS trades. Best risk-adjusted by a wide margin.
pros: Lowest OOS MaxDD across all winners
cons: Likely to overlap heavily with research/21 — both are Donchian-style breakouts on same universe

items.label: ADX trend — adx_30 (highest CAGR)
items.body: PF 1.77, Sharpe 1.30, MaxDD 16.47%, CAGR +15.50%, n=225 OOS trades. Strict ADX>30 — different entry context than fresh breakouts.
pros: Highest OOS CAGR + Sharpe of all winners
Captures established trends rather than fresh breaks
Strong IS->OOS Sharpe lift (0.51 -> 1.30) suggests robustness
cons: adx_25_pure variant overfit badly (IS PF 2.57 -> OOS 1.22) — adx_30 only narrowly survived; small parameter shifts kill it

items.label: Pullback — pb_rsi40 (most non-correlated)
items.body: PF 1.55, Sharpe 1.01, MaxDD 18.19%, CAGR +12.99%, n=233 OOS trades. RSI<40 in established uptrend (50>200 SMA).
pros: Buy-the-dip vs breakout — most non-correlated entry style of the three
Largest OOS sample of the three
MACD/confluence variants underperformed — minimal-filter version won, lower overfit risk
cons: MaxDD 18% is highest of the three winners
RSI-driven mean-reversion-in-trend is more sensitive to whipsaw than pure breakout

sections.title: Hard verdicts from the sweep
items: 24 of 24 SHORT-direction variants failed (bearish breakdown 5, death cross 6, ADX-SHORT 5, pullback-SHORT 5, RS-low 3 implicit). F&O 76 universe is structurally long-biased — bear-put debit spreads from these signals lose money. Drop SHORT side entirely.
Confluence/extra-filter variants underperformed across the board — minimal-filter winners had higher OOS PF in 5 of 6 families. The 'every approach needs supporting filters' hypothesis didn't hold.

Second-tier passers (RS leadership rs_60d_z2.0, golden cross gc_ema_20_50) are deployable but RS overlaps heavily with Donchian 55 entry context, and gc_ema_20_50 has 24% MaxDD — close to the 30% gate. Skip these.
OOS regime (2023-25) was a strong Indian bull market. Bear-regime stress (2008-10, 2020-Q1) is the missing test before scaling capital.

sections.title: Deploy plan when we revisit
items: Step 1: Run signal-overlap analysis. For each of 3 winners, compute % of trade-days where it co-fires with research/21 fno system. If overlap > 60%, treat it as a filter on existing fno, not a new system.

Step 2: Add surviving signals as new system_ids in services/eod_breakout_scanner.py. Plumbing precedent: SYSTEM_NIFTY500/SMALLCAP/FNO already wired — same pattern. New ids: 'donchian55', 'adx30', 'pullback_rsi40'.

Step 3: Each new system_id auto-inherits the spread-structure layer (services/spread_structure.py builds bull-call on signal-fire, scanner persists JSON, EodBreakout.tsx renders strikes/debit/max-profit/RR + adjustment playbook).
Step 4: Paper-only for 30 trading days. Track per-system PF/Sharpe/DD against backtest expectations.
Step 5: Promote whichever survives the paper period to live with debit-spread sizing — risk = max_loss = net debit per spread.

sections.title: Artifacts
items: Status doc: docs/FNO-SPREAD-DEPLOY-STATUS.md (full result tables)
Per-signal folders: research/22_fno_bearish_breakdown/ through research/27_fno_pullback_trend/
Each contains: results/summary.csv (full sweep), results/walk_forward.csv (top variants), results/equity_*.csv, results/trades_<best>.csv, SIGNAL-STATUS.md (verdict)
Shared engine: research/22_fno_bearish_breakdown/scripts/_shared_engine.py (~430 lines, direction-aware, used by all 6 signal folders)

plans.title: Small/Micro-Cap Daily ATH/52w Breakout System
plans.subtitle: Walk-forward VALIDATED. OOS: PF 1.46 / Sharpe 1.43 / MaxDD 12.85% / CAGR +26.8% on 117 quality-filtered small/micro-caps. Sister to research/17.

sections.title: Pivot — daily bars, not intraday
sections.body: Earlier intraday version of this plan was BLOCKED by a data gap: local 5-min DB contains only Nifty 500 large/mid-caps, no genuine small/micro-cap 5-min bars. Pivoting to DAILY bars dodges the data problem entirely (we have 1,623 daily-bar symbols since 2018+) and aligns with the research/17 EOD scanner's winning structural pattern (PF 1.44 OOS / Sharpe 0.95 OOS on Nifty 500). This iteration applies the same family of rules to a smaller-cap universe with stricter quality filters.

items.label: Breakout trigger
items.body: Today's close > 252-day high (52-week breakout) OR today's close > all-time high in our data window. Both compute identically against rolling-max-excluding-today; use 252-day as primary since most stocks have <8 yrs in DB.

items.label: Volume confirmation
items.body: Today's volume >= 2.5x trailing 50-day average (stricter than research/17's 2.0x — small caps have noisier baseline volume, need higher conviction).

items.label: Regime filter
items.body: Today's close > 200-day SMA (only buy in confirmed uptrend on the stock itself; bear-market protection inherits same logic as research/17).

items.label: Execution timing
items.body: Signal triggers at EOD close. Enter at NEXT day's open (same operational pattern as research/17).

sections.title: Universe selection — quality filters before backtest

items.label: Step 1 — base universe
items.body: All daily-bar symbols in market_data_unified (~1,623). Filter to stocks with >=1500 daily bars since 2018-01-01. Exclude Nifty 500 (already covered by research/17) — focus on small/micro-cap exposure.

items.label: Step 2 — minimum market cap proxy via turnover
items.body: Min avg daily turnover Rs 5 Cr over trailing 60 days (liquidity floor for actual execution). Below this, even small position sizes face 0.5%+ impact cost.

items.label: Step 3 — maximum turnover cap (keep it small/micro)
items.body: Max avg daily turnover Rs 100 Cr over trailing 60 days — ensures we're not just re-testing mid-caps already in research/17 universe.

items.label: Step 4 — circuit / freeze filter (red flag check)
items.body: Exclude stocks where >5% of last 60 trading days hit a daily move >=10% (proxy for circuit hits / news-driven manipulation / illiquid pricing). These stocks have execution risk: positions get stuck when circuit triggers.

items.label: Step 5 — series filter
items.body: Keep only stocks in the 'EQ' (regular cash) series. Exclude T2T (trade-to-trade), Z series, BE series — these have settlement constraints incompatible with a swing entry/exit pattern.

items.label: Step 6 — volatility floor
items.body: Min avg ATR-as-pct-of-price >= 1% (exclude completely range-bound dead stocks).

items.label: Stop loss
items.body: max(entry - 2 * ATR(14), entry * 0.92) — whichever closer to entry. Caps day-1 risk at 8%.

items.label: Target — fixed 25%
items.body: Entry × 1.25. No trailing stop, no time stop except 60-day max-hold safety. Same logic as research/17 D1_fixed_25_8 (the OOS-validated winner).

items.label: Why fixed 25% (not trailing)
items.body: Research/17 walk-forward proved fixed targets dominate trailing stops on daily-bar momentum: 41-day avg hold lets winners run to full 25%, trail-based exits get whipped on retracements before the move completes.

items.label: Risk per trade
items.body: 1% of equity. Quantity = floor((equity * 0.01) / (entry - stop)).

items.label: Max concurrent positions
items.body: 10 (same as research/17). On Rs 10L capital, max Rs 1L notional per position.

items.label: Costs
items.body: 0.30% round-trip (vs research/17's 0.20% — small caps have wider spreads). Stress test at 0.50%.

items.label: Capital allocation
items.body: Rs 10,00,000 initial. Long-only (small-cap shorts via cash impossible; F&O futures unavailable on this universe). Skips short signal handling entirely.

sections.title: Backtest scope
sections.body: Period 2018-01-01 to 2025-12-31 (8 years). Bar TF: daily. Capital: Rs 10L. Walk-forward train 2018-2022 / test 2023-2025 (matches research/17 split for direct comparison).

sections.title: Pass criteria
items: OOS Profit Factor >= 1.20 (same as research/17)
OOS Sharpe >= 0.8
OOS MaxDD <= 30% (same as research/17 — small-cap MaxDD will likely be higher than mid-cap, but we accept that for the higher CAGR potential)
Trade frequency: 30-100 trades/year across the curated universe

sections.title: Why this should work (a-priori thesis)
sections.body: Research/17 proved daily ATH/52w breakouts + volume + regime filter is structurally edge-positive on Nifty 500. Small-caps amplify the move size when they break out (higher beta of single-stock momentum), but also amplify the noise (worse spreads, more news-driven false breakouts). The strict quality filters (turnover, circuit history, EQ series, volatility floor) should remove enough noise to leave the genuine edge. Net expectation: similar PF/Sharpe to research/17 (1.4 / 0.9), potentially higher CAGR, slightly higher MaxDD.

plans.title: ORB Directional Options — Nifty
plans.subtitle: 5-min directional options overlay on the existing ORB cash signal

sections.title: Goal
sections.body: When the ORB signal fires on any of the 15 cash stocks, mirror that directional view on NIFTY via options. Exploit the already-computed ORB conviction to size an options trade without taking separate directional risk on the index. Paper-only until backtest confirms edge.

items.label: Variant A · Pure naked option sell
items.body: On SHORT signal → SELL ATM or slightly-OTM NIFTY CE. On LONG signal → SELL ATM or slightly-OTM NIFTY PE.
pros: Collect full premium if direction is right
cons: Unbounded risk on the wrong-side tail

items.label: Variant B · Directional strangle
items.body: On SHORT signal → SELL closer-ATM CE + SELL far-OTM PE. On LONG signal → SELL closer-ATM PE + SELL far-OTM CE. Far-leg acts as tail hedge and improves margin.
pros: Lower net margin via spread benefit
Extra premium from the hedge leg

items.label: Variant C · Conviction-ranked router
items.body: Route grade A+/A signals to Variant A (naked — high conviction) · Route B/C to Variant B (hedged) · Skip anything with no stars.
pros: Uses existing conviction rubric already saved on positions
Right risk profile for each signal quality
cons: Needs sample size to confirm grade-vs-outcome correlation

items.label: 1. Hard SL at 2× entry premium
items.body: Classic short-premium rule. Max loss = initial premium collected. Place as exchange SL-L with trigger at 2× entry, limit at 2.1× entry.

items.label: 2. SuperTrend trail on NIFTY spot
items.body: Exit the option when NIFTY spot SuperTrend flips against the trade. Test ST(7, 2.0) and ST(7, 3.0) — narrower band (2.0) catches smaller reversals but may whipsaw.

items.label: 3. Time-based exit
items.body: Candidates: 14:30 (aggressive) · 15:15 (standard EOD) · 15:20 (Zerodha MIS auto-squareoff). Test whether 14:30 loses the late-afternoon intrinsic decay edge.

items.label: 4. Partial scale-out
items.body: Mirror the ORB R-multiple style: exit 50% of lots at +30% premium decay (i.e. option value drops to 0.7× entry) · let remainder ride till SL or ST-flip.

sections.title: Open design questions
items: Strike selection: fixed-offset from spot? LTP-based search targeting premium ~Rs 80? Delta-based (e.g. short the 0.30 delta)?
Capital allocation: 1 lot per ORB signal? Scaled by conviction grade? Capped by current MIS margin headroom?
Concurrent cap: how many parallel option positions? 1? 3? 5?
Expiry choice: current-week for max theta + max gamma, or next-week for smoother decay?
SL placement: exchange-side SL-L on the option leg, or monitor-driven 2× check every tick?
Direction conflict: what if two ORB stocks give opposite signals? Take both (opposing NIFTY options), net out, or pick by conviction?

sections.title: Prerequisites before backtesting
items: Need 20+ trading days of captured 1-min options data (currently 3 sessions)
Reconstruct 5-min NIFTY candles from 1-min data for the ST calculation
Wire the ORB signal bus to emit signals to a second consumer (options engine)
Build a simple backtester using the captured option_chain snapshots

sections.title: Integration notes
items: Reuses the existing ORB signal engine 1:1 — no new signal logic
Fires at the same moment the cash ORB trade fires (so same filters apply)
Conviction grade already saved on orb_positions — direct lookup
New DB: backtest_data/orb_options.db (positions / orders / pnl)
Keep in paper mode (nas-style) until backtest shows edge
